phase_name: Session 40: Hodge dead end -> barrier constant -> determinant reduction of RH

completed_tasks.name: KILL: Hodge-Riemann on null(W02) is trivially satisfied (M neg-def)
DISCOVER: barrier ~0.04 on range(W02), constant across 3 orders of magnitude
DISCOVER: 2x2 block is diagonal (parity), no range-range mixing
DISCOVER: barrier = (W02-M_prime) - M_diag - M_alpha, 3 log-slopes cancel to 3.5%
DISCOVER: 4x4 effective matrix, determinant reduction of RH

remaining_tasks.name: Prove QW_barrier -> c > 0 from Weil explicit formula
Track the 4 determinant quantities (barrier, QW_null, M_cross, det) vs lambda^2
Derive closed-form asymptotics for each barrier component
Investigate silent modes (M ~ -9e-9 on null) — do they stay negative?

blockers.description: Numerical precision limits eps_0 determination at ~10^-10. Need analytical approach for large lambda^2.
Cross-coupling M_cross ~ 0.5 but effect ~ 10^-10. Need to prove this cancellation persists.

decisions.decision: Hodge-Riemann is trivially satisfied on null(W02)
decisions.rationale: M is negative definite there, so Q_W = -M > 0 automatically. Wrong mountain.
Eigenvalues forced into +-lambda pairs. Proved algebraically, verified to 1e-15.
W02 and M both grow as lam^1.5, constant residual ~0.04. Parity decouples to two 1D problems.
RH <=> det(2x2 block) > 0 <=> QW_barrier * QW_null_coupled > M_cross^2. Four explicit sums.

next_action: Prove the barrier constant: derive asymptotic expansion of QW_barrier = <w, W02-M, w> from the Weil explicit formula. Each component (W02, M_prime, M_diag, M_alpha) has a closed-form expression on the odd eigenvector w[n] = n/(L^2 + (4pi)^2 n^2). The 3-slope cancellation (0.257 - 0.193 - 0.073 = -0.009) should follow from the explicit formula structure.
context_notes: Session 40 is the most productive session yet. Started building Hodge star tools, proved an algebraic obstruction that killed the approach, pivoted to the barrier on range(W02), discovered a constant ~0.04 residual from a 4-way cancellation, and reduced RH to a determinant inequality on a 4x4 effective matrix. The barrier anatomy: W02 and M_prime both ~lam^1.5 (cancel), M_diag and M_alpha both ~log(lam) (cancel against the W02-M_prime residual), leaving ~0.04. The 4x4 decouples by parity into two 2x2 blocks, each with determinant condition. Four numbers from the Weil explicit formula. Built 3 Python files, installed dash+plotly.
